symptom: a fall and no memory of ever being sick
hook: dozens of small round white dots were scattered across the spleen like a sky full of stars
whatYouSee: Dozens of small round white dots scattered evenly across the spleen like a sky full of stars.
whyItMatters: A fungal infection years ago left tiny scars that slowly calcified as the body sealed each one off for good.
treatment: Nothing needs treating because these are old healed scars so the finding is simply noted and left alone.
takeaway: A spleen can carry its own night sky and never say a word about it.
igOptions: A. Splenic calcifications from an old healed infection
C. Kidney stones overlying the spleen

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a fall and no memory of ever being sick.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And dozens of small round white dots were scattered across the spleen like a sky full of stars.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Splenic calcification (histoplasmosis)

👀 What you see:
Dozens of small round white dots scattered evenly across the spleen like a sky full of stars.

🦴 Why it matters:
A fungal infection years ago left tiny scars that slowly calcified as the body sealed each one off for good.

💊 Treatment:

Nothing needs treating because these are old healed scars so the finding is simply noted and left alone.
generated.ctaText:

I put 5 of the strangest into a free pack.

laypersonQuestion: Why would an organ look like a starry night?
seedHint: Count the dots on the left side and ask yourself if they grew there or fell there.
